title: ‘Greatest arms I’ve ever seen’: Why Matt Damon had a female stunt double in ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Entertainment Matt Damon underwent a physical transformation for his role in "The Odyssey," but for one particular action scene, he used a female stunt double.
Matt Damon is Odysseus in "The Odyssey," written, produced, and directed by Christopher Nolan.

Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal By Kevin Slane July 23, 2026 | 12:47 PM 1 minute to read Matt Damon underwent a physical transformation for his role in Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey,” dropping 15 pounds and building muscle by working out five days a week and tweaking his diet.

title: Celtics, Jordan Walsh reportedly agree to three-year contract extension
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Walsh's deal will keep him under contract through 2030.
Jordan Walsh has become a staple of the Celtics rotation this season.
AP Photo/Charles Krupa Celtics forward Jordan Walsh has agreed to a three-year, $16 million contract extension with the franchise, according to “NBA on Prime’s” Chris Haynes.

title: ‘Should I just close everything?’: Why a restaurant owner’s tearful video went viral
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Food News After opening six restaurants in six years, Phi Pham says the emotional video he posted at his lowest moment sparked an outpouring of support from fellow small business owners.
Boston restaurateur Phi Pham went viral last week after sharing a raw, emotional look at the struggles of running a small business — or, in his case, six.
Pham posted a video with the caption, “Should I just close everything?

title: WBZ-TV reporter announces her departure from the station
author: John Waller
bullets: Media "I’m so excited for where the journey takes me next.
" By John Waller July 23, 2026 | 12:21 PM 1 minute to read After about two years, WBZ-TV reporter Samantha Chaney announced last Friday that it was her last day with the station.
“All I can say is: thank you, thank you, thank you!

title: South Station Starbucks reopens after passing inspection following rodent infestation
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The coffee shop passed a city inspection Wednesday with no violations after a TikTok showed mice inside a display case.
Starbucks in South Station sat closed due to rodents.

Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff The gates were open once again Thursday morning at the Starbucks inside South Station, where the coffee shop welcomed customers after a temporary closure over rodent activity.

title: Review & setlist: ‘Stranger Things’ star Finn Wolfhard trades Demogorgons for demos in Boston
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Concert Reviews Wolfhard blended garage punk with Americana sensibilities at the Royale, to the delight of his screaming female fans.
Finn Wolfhard had guitar in hand at the Royale in Boston Wednesday night.

title: Former WCVB reporter Matt Reed to join NECN as anchor for upcoming relaunch
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Media After being purchased by Spectrum, NECN is relaunching as a regional news network this fall.
As NECN prepares to relaunch under new ownership this fall, a well-known local news reporter has confirmed that he is joining the network as an anchor.
Matt Reed, a former WCVB reporter who recently left that station, is set to join Spectrum News NECN as a news anchor, The Boston Globe reported.
